conflict_type: Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and Content Constraints
seed_instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days.
org_constraint: The email must include at least two reasons for requesting the extension.
new_constraint: The email must include the phrase "I do not have any specific reasons for requesting the extension."
instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days. The email must include at least two reasons for requesting the extension. The email must include the phrase "I do not have any specific reasons for requesting the extension."
response:
Subject: Urgent Request for Extension: [Project Name] – [Your Full Name]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well.

I am writing to respectfully request a short extension on the [Project Name], which is currently due in two days. Unfortunately, I am facing some unexpected challenges that are preventing me from completing the project to the standard required.

First, I encountered a significant technical issue with the software I was using to compile the data, which caused me to lose several hours of progress. Second, I underestimated the complexity of the research phase and realized halfway through that I needed to gather additional sources to ensure the quality of my analysis.

Please note that I do not have any specific reasons for requesting the extension, as I am simply asking for the flexibility to resolve these unforeseen obstacles and submit a high-quality assignment.

I am confident that I can complete the project by [Proposed New Date, e.g., tomorrow evening] and would greatly appreciate the opportunity to submit it then. Thank you for your time and understanding.

Sincerely,

[Your Full Name]  
[Your Student ID Number]  
[Course Name/Number]
